chapter_info.subject: Social Science
chapter_info.chapter_name: The Constitution of India - An Introduction

flashcards.q: What is the simplest definition of a constitution?
flashcards.a: A document that spells out a nation's basic principles and laws.

flashcards.q: Where is the original handwritten copy of the Indian Constitution preserved?
flashcards.a: In a helium-filled glass case in Parliament.

flashcards.q: Which gas is used to safely preserve the original Constitution?
When was the Constituent Assembly formed?
Who was the Chairman of the Constituent Assembly?
Who was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee?
On which date was the Constitution of India formally adopted?
On which date did the Constitution come into effect?
How many parts and schedules does the world's largest written constitution have today?
Name the female member of the Constituent Assembly who spoke about women's equality as a long-cherished Indian ideal.
What are the three organs of the Indian government?
Which branch of government is responsible for implementing laws?
What is the three-tier system of government in India?
flashcards.a: Central, State, and Local (Panchayati Raj) levels.

flashcards.q: What does 'Sovereign' mean in the Preamble?
flashcards.a: The supreme right of the people to make decisions without external dictates.

flashcards.q: What does 'Secular' mean in the Preamble?
flashcards.a: No official religion; the government treats all beliefs with equal respect.

flashcards.q: What does 'Fraternity' refer to in the Constitution?
flashcards.a: Behaving as members of the same family with mutual respect.

flashcards.q: Which part of the Constitution is legally enforceable in court if violated?
What serves as the 'guidelines' or long-term social and economic vision for the government?
In which year were 'Fundamental Duties' added to the Constitution?
What term describes the Constitution's ability to be updated over time?
What are formal changes made to the Constitution called?
From which country were the Directive Principles of State Policy inspired?
From which country's constitution were the ideals of 'Liberty, Equality, and Fraternity' adopted?
Who was the calligrapher who wrote the original Constitution by hand?
Who was the artist responsible for illustrating the pages of the original Constitution?
